name: Cape Cod Clam Chowder
recipeIngredient: 24 chowder clams (ground liquor and all through a medium blade of food grinder)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook the bacon in a large kettle until it is crisp, then remove with a slotted spoon.
2. In the same kettle, sauté the onions over medium heat until they are golden brown.
3. Add potatoes, salt, oregano, and basil to the kettle. Pour in boiling water so that it covers the ingredients by about an inch.
4. Let the mixture simmer for 15 minutes or until the potatoes are tender.
5. Stir in the ground clams and cook for a few more minutes.
6. Strain the broth through a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, reserving the liquid.
7. The next day, reheat the reserved broth in a large pot over medium heat.
8. Add milk, butter, salt, and pepper to taste. Stir until heated through.
